title: Seven Protesters Killed Including Shayan Asadollahi
detail: At least seven protesters were killed on the fourth day of unrest, including 28-year-old barber Shayan Asadollahi whose death became a rallying symbol for demonstrators. Lorestan and Ilam provinces, home to Kurdish and Luri minorities, saw the deadliest repression. Between December 31 and January 3, at least 28 people were killed in 13 cities across 8 provinces.

media.caption: Seven protesters killed including Shayan Asadollahi, whose death became a rallying symbol

title: Government Orders Closures in 21 of 31 Provinces
detail: The Iranian government ordered broad closures of businesses, universities, and public spaces in 21 of 31 provinces, citing security concerns. The order effectively acknowledged the regime's inability to contain the protests through force alone. Banks and government offices operated under reduced hours with heavy security cordons.

media.caption: Government orders closures in 21 of 31 provinces as protests overwhelm security forces

title: Protests Reach All 31 Provinces
detail: Despite internet shutdowns and a heavy security presence, protests spread to all 31 Iranian provinces by the end of December 31. Telephone and mobile internet cuts were reported in major cities. International observers noted this was the fastest geographic spread of protest activity in Iran's modern history, surpassing both the 2019 and 2022 movements.

media.caption: Protests reach all 31 Iranian provinces in fastest geographic spread in the country's modern history

title: Deadliest Repression Targets Minority Regions
detail: Lorestan and Ilam provinces, predominantly Kurdish and Luri areas, experienced the most severe security force response. Witnesses reported indiscriminate gunfire in Kuhdasht, Khorramabad, and Ilam city. Communication blackouts made verifying casualty figures extremely difficult, but exile-based human rights groups documented at least 15 deaths in these two provinces alone.

media.caption: Deadliest repression targets Kurdish and Luri minority regions in Lorestan and Ilam provinces
